layman_headline: Crypto market is in fear mode — no strong setups worth risking capital on right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is fearful with a Fear & Greed score of 28. Bitcoin is hovering around $63,000 and its dominance is high at 56%, which means money is flowing out of smaller coins (altcoins) and into Bitcoin. All the altcoin candidates we reviewed have bearish chart structures, weak technicals, or poor risk/reward ratios. Jumping in now would be catching falling knives rather than buying quality setups.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't shown a clear reversal signal yet. Watch for fear to stabilize or for Bitcoin to find firm footing — that's when altcoin opportunities will emerge with better odds.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate suffers from high BTC dominance (56.1%) draining altcoin liquidity. JTO has a slightly bullish chart structure (+0.40 bias) with price above two MAs and a 1.26x risk/reward to nearest levels — the best among candidates. However, RSI at 59 after a +7.14% daily candle indicates the easy money in this bounce may already be captured. ETHFI has a higher chart bias (+0.60) but its risk/reward to resistance is abysmal at 0.11x — resistance at $0.40 is literally upon it. ZRO and TRUMP are oversold (RSI 31 and 29 respectively) but both have bearish chart structures with price below all MAs, making them low-conviction knife catches. The Fear & Greed index at 28 with an improving trend is a positive macro signal historically (80% win rate in our data), but we need a clearer technical trigger before deploying capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.1% continues to suppress altcoin performance — could worsen if BTC breaks below $62K
No volume data available for any candidate, making it impossible to confirm breakouts or accumulation
Geopolitical risk from U.S.-Iran strikes could trigger a broader risk-off selloff
watch_list: BTC holding $62,000 support — a break below would drag all alts further down
Fear & Greed improving past 35 would confirm a sentiment shift worth acting on
JTO pulling back to $0.60 support with a bounce candle would create an ideal entry
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear state (28/100) with BTC dominance high at 56.1%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. Bitcoin ETF inflows are a bright spot, but BTC price action remains weak with geopolitical headwinds from U.S.-Iran tensions.
context_banner: Fear is improving but not yet resolved — capital preservation is the priority until altcoin technicals confirm a bottom.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from 24.6 weekly average — historically reliable signal (80% win rate) but needs technical confirmation before acting.
Bitcoin ETFs drew $197M inflows snapping an 8-week outflow streak — mildly bullish macro signal for eventual altcoin recovery.
Best candidates (TRUMP RSI 29, ZRO RSI 31) are oversold but trapped in bearish structures below all MAs — oversold alone is not enough.
Bitcoin nearing power law support tracked by Fidelity since 2015 — suggests BTC may find a floor soon which would stabilize alt markets.
No candidate shows a bullish chart bias above +0.60, and most are bearish — the setup quality across the board is poor for swing entries.
